data.title: Trigon Metals files Q1 financial results for period ended June 30, 2025. Available on SEDAR+ and Trigon's website.
data.description: In Toronto, Trigon Metals Inc. (TSX-V: TM, OTCPK: PNTZF) has released its condensed consolidated interim financial statements and management's discussion and analysis for the three-month period ending June 30, 2025. The Q1 financial statement and MD&A can be accessed on SEDAR+ at www.sedarplus.ca and on the company's website.

data.title: In Q1 2026, Raoul Pal predicts leading altcoins will rise from a 'waiting room,' extending the crypto market cycle.
data.description: Several prominent alternative cryptocurrencies are anticipated to emerge from a period of stagnancy, which could lead to an extension of the cryptocurrency market cycle into the beginning of 2026 as predicted by Pal.

data.title: Global pharmaceutical contract manufacturing market to reach US$311.95 billion by 2030 with 8.2% CAGR, valued at US$209.90 billion in 2025.
data.description: In Delray Beach, Florida, as of August 29, 2025, the global Pharmaceutical Contract Manufacturing Market saw impressive growth. Starting at a value of US$193.99 billion in 2024, the market reached US$209.90 billion in 2025. Projections indicate that it will continue to thrive, with an expected strong Compound Annual Growth Rate (CAGR) of 8.2% from 2025 to 2030. This growth trajectory is anticipated to lead to significant developments in the industry in the coming years.

data.title: DEMIRE's bond 2019/2027 receives 'B' rating from Fitch, with the issuer rating of ‘CCC+’; ISIN: DE000A2YPAK1.
data.description: DEMIRE has received an issuer rating of ‘CCC+’ from Fitch Ratings for the first time, while their bond (ISIN: DE000A2YPAK1) has been assigned a ‘B’ rating by the same agency.

data.title: Joby Aviation acquires Blade Air Mobility's passenger business, gaining access to terminals and flyers in NY and Europe.
data.description: Joby Aviation, Inc. (NYSE:JOBY) based in Santa Cruz, California, has successfully finalized the acquisition of Blade Air Mobility’s passenger business. With this strategic move, Joby aims to expedite its entry into commercial service with its innovative electric air taxis. The acquisition includes Blade’s well-established terminals and dedicated customer base, particularly in prominent regions such as New York and Southern Europe. This deal equips Joby with a robust foundation to launch its quiet, all-electric vertical takeoff and landing (eVTOL) aircraft, leveraging Blade’s network to enhance its market presence and operational efficiency.

data.title: Philippines construction equipment market to reach 6,948 units by 2030, growing at 4.25% CAGR. Top players include Caterpillar, Komatsu, Volvo CE, Hitachi, XCMG, and SANY.
data.description: A report on the Philippines Construction Equipment Market revealed that in 2024, the market was evaluated at 5,413 units. It is anticipated to grow to 6,948 units by 2030, marking a Compound Annual Growth Rate (CAGR) of 4.25%. Leading players in this market include Caterpillar, Komatsu, Volvo CE, Hitachi Construction Machinery, XCMG, and SANY. These companies hold significant market presence and are driving the growth in the construction equipment sector in the Philippines.

data.title: Elastic N.V. (NYSE: ESTC) reports strong Q1 results, beats estimates with earnings of 60 cents per share and sales of $415.29 million. FY2026 guidance raised to adjusted EPS $2.29-$2.35 and sales $1.68 billion-$1.69 billion.
data.description: Elastic N.V. (NYSE: ESTC) exceeded expectations in the first quarter, with earnings per share at 60 cents, surpassing the analyst estimate of 42 cents per share. Their quarterly sales stood at $415.29 million, also surpassing the analyst estimate of $397.20 million. The company has revised its FY2026 guidance, adjusting the expected EPS from $2.24-$2.32 to $2.29-$2.35 and increasing sales guidance from $1.66 billion-$1.67 billion to $1.68 billion-$1.69 billion. CEO Ash Kulkarni commented, "Elastic had a strong Q1, performing above our projections." This information is available on Benzinga.com.

data.title: FDA requires additional MRI scan for Alzheimer's patients on Biogen's Leqembi due to safety concerns. Drug aims to slow disease progression by targeting beta-amyloid plaques. FDA approved in 2023.
data.description: The FDA has mandated an additional MRI scan for Alzheimer's patients using Leqembi (lecanemab) due to safety concerns related to a rare but potentially fatal side effect. Biogen Inc.'s Leqembi, designed to slow Alzheimer's progression in individuals with mild cognitive impairment or early-stage dementia, was granted FDA approval in 2023. This therapy targets beta-amyloid plaques, protein buildup in the brain associated with the disease's advancement. The drug's goal is to lessen these plaques to help maintain cognitive function. For more details, refer to the Benzinga.com website.
